details.text: Derivative: أَسْرَىٰ – يُسْرِي – إِسْراء: To take at night followed by the Harf بِ.

- أَسْرَى in itself carries the meaning of night and to take at night and so the qualification of لَيْلًا (at night) indicates to it being a portion of the night and not the entire night.
- بِعَبْدِهِ – Allah does not say that He took His Messenger or His Prophet at night but rather His ‘Abd (slave). The Israa’ of the Messenger ﷺ was from the greatest acts of honouring him or any human being ever and yet he was given the title of ‘Abd (slave) which we can associate with no status at all. That is because being an ‘Abd is the highest standing before Allah and there is no greater honour than being the ‘Abd of Allah.

Derivative: عَتاد means the bags that you pack when you are going on a particular mission and pack what you particularly need. It took the meaning of to prepare something and here it means that the painful punishment has been prepared particularly or specially for them.
Derivative: قَتُور or قَتْر means البُخْل الشَّديد which is extreme stinginess.
Derivative: مَثْبُور comes from ثُبُور which means death as the result of a poisonous injury. When an injury becomes infected and you are as good as dead or doomed it is said: ثُبُور.
Derivative: لَفَّ literally means to fold in Arabic. لَفِيف is a group of people belonging to different families that come together.

- And the truth we have sent it down, and the truth did come down i.e., the command was issued that this Qur’an would come down with purpose and the entire delivery was completed purposefully and correctly.
Derivative: مُكْثٍ means to wait or remain in wait for something.
- And we revealed it over a long period of time – again indicating to the Qur’an being revealed in parts – one part after another.

- Indicates that it was sent down in intervals in the case where مُكْثٍ indicates to waiting for the right occasion for the verses to be revealed and it also means to recite something slowly عَلَىٰ مُكْثٍ and not rushed.

- They fall down to their chins. Normally when we fall in prostration our foreheads touch the ground first, however, when somebody falls in an uncontrolled manner they can fall on their chins. This demonstrates how overwhelmed they are by the power of the message.
Derivative: إِن is like إِنَّهُ here reflecting the meaning of: “No doubt” or “Absolutely”.

- This verse has five teachings of Tawhid:
1) Al-Hamdu Lillah.
2) The One who did not take a son – relates to Christians.
3) The One who has no partner in the kingdom – relates to the idolaters of Makkah.
4) He had no friend or helper due to weakness. مَنْ here is of Sababiyah with the meaning: “Due to or because of”.
5) And declare His greatness above all greatness.
- Observation: the first verse of This Surah begins with Subhaanallah, the last begins with Al-Hamdu Lillah, then mentions three realities of Laa Ilaaha Illallah, before ending with Allahu Akbar – thus covering the main elements of Dhikr.

- There are two forms of Shirk related to Allah’s status and greatness: One is when something created is raised too high to Allah’s status. And the other is by stating something about Allah which is not befitting of Him and is lowering of His status. The Christians committed the crime and Shirk of elevating creation and the Jews did the crime and Shirk of reducing the status of Allah by stating “Allah’s hand are tired” or “Allah is poor whilst we are rich”.

Derivative: مَحَوْنَآ
Root: م ح و – مَحَا – يَمْحُو: It means to erase where no trace of it remains.
Derivative: أَلْزَمَ is used when you make something hang, cling or stick to something.

Derivative: طائر
- The literal meaning is bird (طَائِر) however it came to be used to mean fate because the Arabs used to use birds for fortune-telling. Another meaning of طَائِر is vulture and from this it came to take the meaning of a bad omen.

Derivative: وِزْرَ is a burden that you walk around with. When you pack your stuff in a sheet and walk around with it, it is called a وِزْرَ. أَوْزَرَ the إِفْعال forة of the verb وَزَرَ means to conceal, like the things that are concealed inside the cloth. As such وِزْرَ refers to the deeds (i.e., burdens) as things that you have to carry with you but you do your best to hide.

Derivative: It comes from أَتْرَفَ – يُتْرِفُ - إتراف and مُتْرَفٌ is the اسْم مَفْعول form. أَتْرَفَ is in the meaning of أَنْعَمَ to bless or bestow favours. However, أَتْرَفَ includes the meaning that so much blessing was given that the person became blinded by the blessings so that he became misguided. مُتْرَفٌ is the one who has enjoyed these blessings and become corrupted by them and are like the financial elite of the society.
Derivative: To command but here its meaning is to compel.
when allah wishes to destroy a nation, he allows the corrupted financial elite to take charge of its affairs.

- There are two Qiraa’ah of أَمَرْنَا and the second is أَمَّرْنَا with a Shaddah on the middle radical (the Meem). The first meaning of the phrase with أَمَرْنَا is that “We compelled its corrupt financial elite or those corrupted by wealth” whilst the meaning with أَمَّرْنَا is that “We gave authority to the financial elite or those corrupted by wealth” and the result is that they cause further corruption with that authority.

- First Allah mentions خَبِير which means that Allah has full news about what His slaves did but takes it further by stating that His news is not based on a second source but rather based on His direct witnessing بَصِير.
- Another element related to this: What happened already is not seen but rather known about. What happens now is what you see. As such خَبِير captures the nations of the past and بَصِير captures the fact that Allah saw the sins of His slaves in the past just as He sees them in the present.

Derivative: This is the اسْم فاعِل form in the meaning of the مَصْدَر which occurs by adding the تاء المربوطة (ة) to its end. The meaning is: the rush or hastening. It can also be a صِفَة (adjective) where the مَوْصوف (the thing being described) is omitted. In this case that omitted matter could for example refer to the life: الحياة العاجلة with the meaning of: “Whoever desires the rushed or hastened (worldly) life” i.e., its attainment.
Derivative: مَّدْحُورًا comes from دَحَرَ which means to push or kicked out or in this case kicked into hellfire.
Derivative: مَذْمُوم comes from ذَمَّ which means to be condemned for something evil or despicable. مَذْمُوم is the one worthy of condemnation.
Derivative: It means much appreciated, thanked and acknowledged.
Derivative: سَعَىٰ means to make efforts, to rush, run (towards something).

- The ضَمير (pronoun) ها in سَعْيَهَا returns to الْآخِرَةَ (the hereafter). This means he didn’t just put effort towards it but put effort recognising its worth indicating that the person’s efforts correspond to his conviction in what he wants to attain.
details.arabic_part: وَمَنْ   أَرَادَ   الْآخِرَةَ   وَسَعَىٰ   لَهَا   سَعْيَهَا

details.text: Derivative: وَكِيل from وَكَلَ which means to put your trust in someone with a personal matter, to rely on them completely and also delegate that matter to that person to deal with under their discretion.

Derivative: مَحْظُورًا
Root: مَحْظُورًا is from حَظَرَ which means to fence something off to protect it.
Derivative: مَحْظُورًا means to be fenced off or denied.
Derivative: A grant from Allah or the means for acquiring provision.
Derivative: دَرَجَٰتٍ the plural of دَرَجَةٌ and it means rank as opposed to دَرَكَة where دَرَجَةٌ is a higher and higher rank and دَرَكَة is a lower and lower rank.

- The normal principle of grammar is that when you add a Tamyeez it is Mufrad (singular) like for example أَكْثَرُ مالاً (greater in wealth) and here it would be أَكْبَرُ دَرَجَةً. However, here it is أَكْبَرُ دَرَجَٰتٍ which is an eloquent play on words. If it stated دَرَجَةٌ it would mean that the hereafter is greater in rank in the case where there is one ranking system. By stating أَكْبَرُ دَرَجَٰتٍ Allah is stating that the hereafter is even more vast in terms of multitudes of ranks
Derivative: Deceived by a friend. خَذول is someone who appears to be your friend but at the time of need he disappears. مَخْذُول is the victim of that friend.

Derivative: تَقْعُدَ You will remain sitting. قُعود means that you were standing and you sat down, to stand around doing nothing and sit around not getting anything done. Remain sitting means that you will stay on the sidelines of life.

Derivative: Originally refers to the old clippings that are lying around, something disgusting, dirt or filth which you look at and say yuck. Uff then got coined as an expression of frustration, disgust or dislike.
Derivative: نَهَر – يَنْهَرُ – نَهَراً: Means for something to splash really hard. From it you get scolding because scolding to the one receiving it feels like being splashed.

- وَبِٱلْوَٰلِدَيْنِ إِحْسَٰنًا - And Ihsaan (إحْسان) with your parents means to exhibit Ihsaan (excellence) to them both, to treat them with Ihsaan, deal with them with Ihsaan. It did not say “be” good to the parents but just “good” to both parents. The verb is omitted which indicates the urgency and importance of the matter.
- Being good to your parents was mentioned straight after the command to exclusively worship Him, thus highlighting the importance of this command. Our relationship with Allah is unconditional and the next unconditional relationship is that with our parents.
- The highest point that you can reach with worship is Ihsaan and the starting point with parents is Ihsaan. Ihsaan is not the highest expectations but rather the lowest. You will be the best you possibly can be to both your parents.

Derivative: رَبَّيَانِى
Root: ر ب و - رَبَّا – يُرَبِّي – تَرْبِيَّة: To ensure growth, to take care of one’s development.
- It has three interpretations: 1) Out of mercy for your parents 2) Because of the mercy that they showed you 3) If you want Allah’s mercy you have to be humble towards them.

- The direct meaning is “Lower the wing of humility to them both” however it is not just humility that is indicated here but rather humility attached to the ability to take flight as represented in the wing of the bird i.e., being humble despite having the ability not to be humble.
- خَفْضُ الجناح – lowering the wing also refers to the bird protecting its nest and here instead of the bird looking after its offspring it relates to the children taking care of their parents.
Derivative: From آبَ and أَوَّبَ which means to come back from a long journey and to return.

Derivative: تَبْذِير differs from إِسْراف in that the latter إِسْراف is spending on something which is essentially legitimate, but it is too excessive. In contrast تَبْذِير is when you have no reason at all to spend the money. Scholars argue that it ca be done with Halal and Haram things.

- The relationship between those who are entitled their rights and to not squander your wealth wastefully, is that the latter has a consequence on the former as care should be taken to make sure all those with a right to your wealth receive it.
- And do not spread the see all around. بَذَّرَ means to take a seed and throw it. It is used for someone who just throws his wealth around and is wasteful in his spending, squandering it.
- Literally the son of the road indicating to someone who travels all the time and you know that he is from outside of town.
- This indicates to every individual and not relatives in general i.e., that every relative needs to be given his right.

- Why are the squanderers (ٱلْمُبَذِّرِينَ) described as being brothers of the Shayateen
#(إِخْوَٰنَ ٱلشَّيَٰطِينِ)? Insights: Shaytan’s agenda is to instil animosity and hatred amongst us (ref: 5:91). One of the ways is through alcohol and gambling. Another way is to encourage Tabdheer (تَبْذِير) and for the rich to show off their wealth.

Derivative: مَغْلُولَةً
-Derivative: غُلَّ – مَغْلولٌ: To be tied and chained. The Arabs say: Don’t tie your hand to your neck and they say: His hands are tied (غُلَّتْ يَدَهُ). The meaning of the hand being tied to the neck is to be tight-fisted where every time he is needed to spend it feels like he is choking himself and very uncomfortable.

Derivative: إِمْلَاق refers to bankruptcy. However, the meaning of أَمْلَكَ the verb of إِمْلَاق means the danger of destruction. As such, it means the fear of the danger of your wealth being destroyed.

- Allah states “don’t go near Zina” here and not “don’t do Zina”. It includes anything that could facilitate Zina linked to breaking the social boundaries between men and women.
- It is also understood that Zina is the consequence of going near to it i.e., once you take steps to go close to it by breaking some boundaries, Zina would be an expected consequence.
Derivative: قِسْطَاسِ is the tool that ensures Qist; that irregularity does not happen.
Derivative: كَيْل means weight, size and abstract measuring like time.

Derivative: One of the meanings of تَأْوِيل is interpretation. The literal meaning of تَأْوِيل is to make something reach its conclusion. Interpretation comes from this in terms of referring to the conclusion of a speech. Here it refers to “outcome and consequences”.
Derivative: There are two types of عِلْمٌ (knowledge): Revealed and acquired.
Derivative: القَفَا means الظَّهْر – the back. قَفَا the verb means to get right behind somebody, to follow them as if you are almost on their back, to follow them closely (blindly).

Derivative: Your overwhelmed heart driven by passion or emotion. Another meaning for فُؤَاد can be derived from فَئيد which the Arabs say to refer to the final product like after something has been cooked. They will describe it as فَئيد. Based upon this فُؤَاد can refer to the highest form of intellect where the intellect cooks the information and provides you with the final product.
Derivative: مَرَحٌ is شَدَّةُ الفَرَحِ which means overjoyed or an arrogant show of joy (pompousness).
Derivative: خَرْقَة is a piece of cloth that is ripped off.
Derivative: Literally: “You will never be able to break or crack the earth open (by your arrogant pompous walking)” which effectively means: Know your place and limits.
Derivative: مَكْرُوه when mentioned in the Qur’an is not the same as its meaning in Fiqh and is much stronger. It means something that is detested, hated and unacceptable to Allah.
the usage of إِلَيْكَ here implies that the address is to the prophet ﷺ and carries a threat. the purpose is to demonstrate that the warning of allah applies equally to all his slaves.

Derivative: لَتَعْلُنَّ
Root: عَلا - ع ل و – يَعْلُو – عُلُوّ: Means to be high but also means to raise your head in rebellion. It also means to attain great power.
Derivative: صَرَّفَ – تَصْريف: means to alternate, to explain in multiple various ways.
Derivative: نُفُور means hatred. That is why نَفير refers to a military group that goes out against its enemy out of hate.

Derivative: حَليم means forbearing. It is like صابِر (patience) but has both and element of patience and love in it. حِلْم is the kind of love that a mother shows to a child especially when the child has been disobedient.

- Here إِن of negation (إِن نافٍيَة) is used. In Arabic you could use ما of negation to state: مَا مِنْ شَيْء so, what is the difference between مَا and إِن here. ما is used for refutation whereas here it is not a refutation. There is no scope for debate but just a matter of fact. You are not being responded to but rather being informed.

حِجَاب means a barrier: A means to keep something from reaching somewhere. مَسْتُور is the اِسْم المَفْعول (Ism ul-Maf’ul) from سَتَرَ which means to cover. The difference between حِجاب and سَتَر is that سَتَر does not complete cover but almost entirely covers. حِجَاب is a Masdar and can come here implying Mubaalaghah (exaggeration and emphasis). In the case where مَسْتُور is اِسْم المَفْعول (Ism ul-Maf’ul) it can mean that the حِجَاب (Hijab) is مَسْتُور (covered up) i.e., that the barrier is almost entirely invisible.
Derivative: أَكِنَّةً comes from كَنَّ which means to keep away from the sun. As such أَكِنَّةً portrays a seal that prevents the light from reaching them.
Derivative: A barrier like a load and burden that blocks.

Derivative: نَجْوَىٰٓ is a مَصْدَر. نَجْوَىٰٓ is also a صِفَة (adjective) that doesn’t change in the singular, dual or plural forms. It means to make a conspiracy, to conspire and the one who makes the conspiracy. نَجْوَىٰٓis related in root origin to the word نَجَاة which means rescue and saving. As such نَجْوَىٰٓ is a conspiracy to save yourself from somebody by conspiring against him.
Derivative: رُفات means that which has become rotten, dissolved and mixed into the dirt and scattered away (like dust).

Derivative: جَاسَ means to invade forcefully and to ravage something (like a house). It also means to go in the middle of two things that are combined, like a toothpick between the teeth where space is tight. It also means to get right into the middle of something.
Derivative: It means between like بَيْنَ but really means between two very tight spaces.
- This combination of words means that nothing and nobody was spared to the extent that the violence and killing penetrated inside the nooks and crannies of the very homes of the people.
Derivative: أَنْغَضَ – يُنْغِضُ: To move your head up and down in derision and mockery.
Derivative: نَزْغ is friction created between two parties.

- Allah states that He gave preference to some Prophets over others but left it at that without stating a ranking list or specifically stating some over others. That is because it takes the focus away from Allah.
details.arabic_part: وَلَقَدْ فَضَّلْنَا بَعْضَ ٱلنَّبِيِّۦنَ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ

details.text: Derivative: مَحْذُور comes from حَذِرَ which means to take caution. The meaning is therefore: That which has been cautioned about. That which you have should beware.
Derivative: وَسِيلَةَ is a means of getting closer.
- The addition of كَانَ to مَحْذُورًا indicates that all revelations of the past warned about Allah’s punishment.
Derivative: مَسْطُورًا comes from سَطر : To line in, documented line by line in the book.

- نَفِير means a small battalion that is ready to go and fight. Instead of saying أَكْثَرَ عَدَداً meaning greater in number or more numerous, the statement of أَكْثَرَ نَفِيرًا implies that we provided you with greater or more military strength.
- Hugely rebellious.
- referring to شَجَرَةُ الزَّقُّوم
Derivative: اِحْتِناك is when a swarm of locusts of insects destroy a crop. Here it means that Shaytan is saying that he will eat away all the good (i.e., of Adam’s offspring).
- The كَاف in أَرَءَيْتَكَ is not the مَفْعُول بِهِ but rather it is كَاف المُبالَغَة (Kaaf of exaggeration) and the meaning is “Do you really or actually see or view?!” with emphasis.
Derivative: وَفْر means to be a lot, to be filled more than necessary even. مَّوْفُور means that you will not be given any reduction but rather given your complete fill (i.e., of punishment).
Derivative: أَجْلَبَ means in relation to horses to march them and drive them forward.
Derivative: رَجِل is the plural of رَاجِل and it refers to foot soldiers.

Derivative: اِسْتفَزَّ – يَسْتَفِزُّ – اِسْتِفْزَاز: It originates from the word فَزَّ which means to intimidate and startle leading to making that person leave. It also refers to the unsteady feet of a newborn goat or cow when it tries to stand up for the first time and can’t stand in its place due to weakness. اِسْتفَزَّ is to try and make somebody wobbly or unsteady in their stance, meaning to make them move from their position.
Derivative: أَزْجَى – يُزْجِي: It means to push to make something move.
Derivative: كَفُورًا here means extremely ungrateful.
Derivative: حَصْباء are pebbles and حَاصِب is a meteor shower, rocks coming from the sky.

Derivative: تَارَةً is one of the three words used in the Qur’an for turn in terms of another time: مَرَّة – كَرَّة – تَارَةً. تَارَةً is used for something that happens a second time again after a time for the same thing to be done or repeated again.
Derivative: قَاصِفًا relates to water damage. It is a storm that destroys everything in its path like a hurricane.
Derivative: تَبِيعٌ is literally someone who follows and here it refers to someone who comes after to avenge.

- The use of the pronoun فَلَهَا for the one who has done bad as opposed to the use of أَنفُسِكُمْ for those who acted well because the doing of bad is disappointing and so it is distanced by using the third person instead of the second person (أَنفُسِكُمْ) for the one who has done good.
details.arabic_part: إِنْ   أَحْسَنتُمْ   أَحْسَنتُمْ   لِأَنفُسِكُمْ  ۖ  وَإِنْ   أَسَأْتُمْ   فَلَهَا

details.text: Derivative: تَبَّرَ means to destroy and annihilate completely so no trace of the enemy is left. Here it means to complete and utterly destroy everything that had been raised and built up in terms of civilisation and heights reached leaving no trace behind.

Derivative: حَمَلْنَٰهُمْ – And We carried them. حَمَلَ is used more in reference to the mother carrying the child. She carries the child with a great level of care and it is this care which is being referred to here where Allah carries us upon the sea and the land.
Derivative: If you take a seed of a date and there’s a line down the middle of it and sometimes a little fibre is coming out from there. That fibre is called فَتِيلٌ.

Derivative: دُلُوكِ
- دَلَكَ means to become loose (دَلْك) and it also means for something to start descending.

the verse covers all five prayers even though it appears that three are mentioned. that is because لِدُلُوكِ ٱلشَّمْسِ إِلَىٰ غَسَقِ ٱلَّيْلِ covers all prayers from zhuhr to ‘ishaa (i.e., four). and fajr is then mentioned to complete the five.
- قُرْءَانَ ٱلْفَجْر is the term used in the Qur’an here for صلاةُ الفَجْر (The Fajr prayer) because it is a prayer in which you are meant to recite a lot of Qur’an.
- The high and elevated station appointed to the Prophet ﷺ. It is the rank that he will take on Judgement Day.
Derivative: A prison, something that surrounds completely and is a constant imprisonment.

- سُلْطَٰنًا نَّصِيرًا – Here Allah did not just state سُلْطان (authority) but told His Prophet to ask Him for سُلْطَٰنًا نَّصِيرًا (an authority that provides support and help). It is because the authority is not just for the purpose of authority alone but is rather linked to the fulfilment of a mission that requires support. He ﷺ seeks an authority that will help him accomplish his mission.
Derivative: زَهَقَ means to run off and disappear. In this case because the Batil (falsehood) is unable to face the Haqq (truth).
Derivative: شِفَآءٌ means cure and according to Surah Yunus (Verse: 57) it is a cure for what lies in the chest (i.e., of diseases like arrogance).
Derivative: شَاكِلَة means predisposition and comes from the word شَكْل which means form and it is indicative of the limitations of each person.

Derivative: ٱلرُّوحُ
- Here it refers to that matter which is inside us of which we have little knowledge but has also been used to describe the revelation, the Qur’an and Jibreel (as). A word associated with this is نُور (light)in the case where: Revelation has been called light, our Fitrah (nature) has been referred to with light and Angels are creations of light.
- The Ruh is from the domain of Al-Amr (the command) of my Rabb (Lord/Master) whereas our physical body is from the domain of Al-Khalq (the creation).
Derivative: ظَهِيرًا means that they got their back, serving as backup (for one another) and helping (each other) from the word ظَهْر (back).
Derivative: صَرَّفَ - to present in variations of ways.
- أَبَى means to refuse. أَبَى إِلا means to be bent and stuck (adamant) upon something.

Derivative: أَقْوَمُ
- The use of أَقْوَمُ (more upright) instead of الأَقْوَمُ (the most upright) guides to a comparison being made to something else i.e., the Tawrah may contain some guidance but the Qur’an is better and will guide you to the more right way.

Derivative: يَنۢبُوعًا
Root: ن ب ع – نَبْعٌ means a spring of water. يَنْبُوع refers to a bubbling spring of water that appears. Originally constructed from يا and نُبوع and includes the sense of shock or astonishment.
Derivative: قَبِيلًا means face to face.
Derivative: كِسَف is a piece of something that is soft like cotton or fiber that slowly falls to the ground.
Derivative: بَشَر a mortal human being or skin (referring to the human).
Derivative: خَبَتْ from خَبَا – يَخْبُو - خَبْو : It means to run out of flame or fire dying down
